rationale: The citing paper references Shu and Gneezy (2010) only once, in a grouped parenthetical citation with Nowlis et al. (2004), to support a general point that less frequent consumption may increase positive anticipation. The citation functions as broad background in the theoretical rationale and is not discussed separately. The paper does not build its hypotheses directly on this specific study, nor does it reuse its method, measures, or core procrastination mechanism.
